title: GTM → Portfolio full journal sync
summary: Implemented phoneHomeToPortfolio() so child GTMs automatically sync full journal content up to their portfolio parent on session end.
detail: Added GTMSyncPayload interface and phoneHomeToPortfolio() function to src/lib/service-gtm.ts. Mirrors the existing phoneHomeToGTM() pattern: reads GTM journal entries since last sync, copies journal files to portfolio with gtm-{name}- prefix, writes aggregated service-sync journal entry to portfolio, updates registered_services last_sync, writes inbox trigger, emits context hub event (non-fatal). Upgraded jfl portfolio phone-home command (src/commands/portfolio.ts) to call phoneHomeToPortfolio() instead of the old lightweight report. Added phone_home_to_portfolio helper to session-cleanup.sh with two call sites (after both successful merge+push paths). Added portfolio sync block to session-end.sh between the push and product sync steps. Build passes cleanly.
next: Test with a real child GTM that has portfolio_parent set

title: visa-portfolio: superhero command center initialized
summary: Built Andrew's master Visa coordination layer at /Users/andrewhathaway/code/visa. Portfolio type with port 4300, visa-superhero and productrank-gtm registered, 13 knowledge files populated including canonical intel extracted from visa-superhero.
detail: Created from scratch: git init, .jfl/config.json (portfolio type, port 4300), .mcp.json (port 4300), .gitignore (excludes all 6 nested workspace repos), CLAUDE.md (custom superhero command center doc, ~7KB). Extracted real knowledge from visa-superhero: VISION, NARRATIVE, THESIS, TEAM, INTEL, PROJECTS, visa-ecosystem, conversations/. Created new portfolio-only files: WORKSPACE_MAP (full path/port/purpose map), ANDREW (Andrew's brief for agents), OPPORTUNITIES (8 prioritized opportunities including CyberSource shell, virtual card MCP, Visa skill files), DECISIONS (master log: DX-001 CyberSource, DX-002 virtual card, TEAM-001 Colorado telecommute, ARCH-001 TAP vs VI, AGENT-001 Allium pattern, CRYPTO-001 x402/USDC), METRICS (ProductRank baselines 0.845/0.844, port map, team context). Ran jfl portfolio register for both GTMs - portfolio_parent backlink written to child configs. Initial commit: 135 files. Git status clean (subdirectories correctly excluded by .gitignore).
next: Open Claude Code in /Users/andrewhathaway/code/visa — the portfolio is ready to use

title: JFL-CLI → Pi migration: packages/pi full implementation
summary: Implemented the complete @jfl/pi package as described in the Pi migration plan. All phases 1-8 implemented in a single session: foundation package, all extensions, skills migration, themes, teams, and CLI integration.
detail: Created packages/pi/ with full structure: (1) package.json with pi-package keyword, tsconfig.json; (2) types.ts — Pi extension type definitions (PiContext, PiLifecycleHooks, PiTool, etc.); (3) index.ts — master entry composing all sub-extensions; (4) session.ts — worktree lifecycle, auto-commit daemon, shell script delegation; (5) map-bridge.ts — Pi lifecycle → MAP event bus HTTP posts with scope enforcement (reads context_scope from .jfl/config.json); (6) context.ts — hub ensure, before_agent_start injection, jfl_context tool; (7) journal.ts — git commit detection, /journal command, footer widget, before_compact check; (8) eval.ts — per-turn eval capture via eval-store.ts; (9) hud-tool.ts — jfl_hud tool, /hud command, aboveEditor widget; (10) crm-tool.ts — delegates to ./crm CLI; (11) memory-tool.ts — Context Hub memory search API; (12) synopsis-tool.ts — synopsis CLI delegation; (13) stratus-bridge.ts — predict before agent_start, resolve after agent_end, training tuples to ~/.jfl/training-buffer.jsonl; (14) peter-parker.ts — full orchestrator replacing WebSocket bridge, subscribes to MAP events, spawns Pi RPC subprocesses, review loop max 5 iterations; (15) portfolio-bridge.ts — phoneHomeToPortfolio on shutdown, journal sync to parent, eval propagation to portfolio hub; (16) agent-grid.ts — /grid command, polls agent:health, live status display. Skills: copied 17 existing skills + created 3 new (pi-agents, eval, context), renamed ralph-tui→orchestrate, updated hud/end/orchestrate for Pi. Themes: jfl.theme.json with 51 design tokens. Teams: gtm-team.yaml (Scout/Planner/Builder/GTM/Content/Eval) and dev-team.yaml. CLI: jfl pi + jfl pi agents run commands. AGENTS.md coordination docs. All TypeScript compiles clean, jfl build passes.
next: Test with actual Pi installation. Wire agent:health emitter into each spawned subprocess. Possibly add nightly cron for training-buffer→Stratus policy head batch in stratus-bridge.ts. Publish as @jfl/pi to npm.
learned: Pi extension API uses named lifecycle hooks (session_start, agent_start, etc.) — clean integration points vs Claude Code hooks
Pi's before_agent_start can return systemPromptAddition for context injection without MCP
scope enforcement in map-bridge is simple: read context_scope from config, filter produces/consumes/denied patterns
